HEIGHT 6'0"  WEIGHT 205 lbs

David Cremin was born on October 15, 1989 in Vicenza, Italy. The native of Annandale, Virginia has a Bachelor's of Science in kinesiology from the University of Massachusetts, Amherst. While at UMASS, Cremin was a member of the crew team. He was a member of the soccer and cross-country teams in high school. His cousin, Steven Langton introduced him to the sport of bobsled after Langton competed in the 2010 Vancouver Winter Olympic Games.

FUN FACTS

- He grew up in a Military family (Army.)
- He enjoys playing guitar and serenading his teammates.
- He is the foremost authority on all things Harry Potter.

CAREER HIGHLIGHTS

  • 2015-2016 World Cup in four-person for pilot Codie Bascue - 15th in Lake Placid (1/16); 19th in Park City #2 (1/16)
  • 2015-2016 North American Cup in four-person for pilot Codie Bascue - 4th in Calgary #1 (11/15); GOLD in Calgary #2 (11/15); 5th in Calgary #3 (11/15); SILVER in Park City #1 (2/16); GOLD in Park City #2 (2/16)
  • 2015 World Championships in four-person for pilot Codie Bascue - 20th (3/15)
  • 2014-2015 World Cup in four-person for pilot Elana Meyers Taylor - 13th in Sochi (2/15)
  • 2014-2015 World Cup in four-person for pilot Nick Cunningham - 13th in Koenigssee (1/15)
  • 2014-2015 World Cup in four-person for pilot Steven Holcomb - 7th in Calgary (12/14); 9th in Altenberg (1/15)
  • 2014-2015 World Cup in four-person for pilot Codie Bascue - 9th in Lake Placid (12/14); 12th in St. Moritz (1/15); 15th in La Plagne (2/15)
  • 2014-2015 North American Cup as brakeman in two-man for pilot Codie Bascue - 6th in Calgary #1 (11/14); BRONZE in Park City #1 (11/14)
  • 2014-2015 North American Cup as brakeman in four-person for pilot Codie Bascue - BRONZE in Calgary #2 (11/14); 4th in Calgary #1 (11/14); 4th in Park City (11/14)
  • 2012-2013 North American Cup as brakeman for Colin Coughlin in two-man

bio.education: Lincoln-West High School (Cleveland, Ohio) Pikes Peak Community College (Colorado Springs, Colo.)
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Dartanyon Crockett was born with Leber's disease, a genetic condition causing progressive vision loss. After his mother died when he was just eight years old, Dartanyon endured a life of transiency and extreme poverty at the mercy of his father who struggled with substance abuse
  • Wrestling became an important outlet for Crockett throughout high school, and it was on the mats that the he met his best friend, Leroy Sutton. Sutton lost his legs in train accident at age eleven, and Crockett physically carried Sutton on his back through practices and competitions. In 2009, ESPN featured the extraordinary friendship between these two young men, inspiring thousands of viewers to lift them out of poverty
  • In 2010, Crockett was invited to try blind judo. Just two years into this new venture, he shocked the world by winning a bronze medal at the London Paralympic Games. Crockett has qualified his weight class for the 2016 Rio Paralympic Games with his victory at last year's IBSA World Championships.
Career Highlights
  • Five-time Senior National Paralympic Champion (2014, 2013, 2012, 2011, 2010)
  • 2015 German Open, Heidelberg, Germany, silver medalist
  • 2014 IBSA World Champion
  • 2014 German Open, silver medalist
  • 2014 International Grand Prix Infraero, San Paulo, Brazil, bronze medalist
  • 2013 U.S. Open, bronze medalist
  • 2012 Paralympics, bronze medalist
  • 2012 Pajulahti Games, gold medalist
  • 2011 Para Pan Games, silver medalist
  • 2016 Bronze Medalist, Paralympic Games, Rio de Janeiro, Brazil
  • 2015 Silver Medalist, German Open for Blind and Visually Impaired, Heidelberg, Germany
  • 2014 Gold Medalist, IBSA World Championships, Colorado Springs, CO
  • 2014 Bronze medalist, International Grand Prix Infraero, San Paulo, Brazil
  • 2012 Bronze medalist, Paralympic Games, London, United Kingdom
  • 2012 Gold Medalist, Pajulahti Games, Nastola, Finland
  • 2011 Team Member, IPC Para Pan American Games, Guadalajara, Mexico
  • 2011 Team Member , IBSA World Games, Antalya, Turkey
  • 2010, 2011, 2012, 2014, 2015, Gold Medalist , USA Judo National Championships for the Blind and Visually Impaired
  • Paralympic Experience: Two-time Paralympian (2012,2016)
  • Paralympic Experience: Rio 2016 Paralympic Games: bronze
  • Paralympic Experience: London 2012 Paralympic Games: bronze
Paralympic Experience
  • 2-time Paralympian; 2-time Paralympic medalist (2 bronze)
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, bronze (90kg - Men)
    • Paralympic Games London 2012, bronze (90kg - Men)
World Championships Experience

  • Years of participation: 90kg - Men 2014

Quick Facts
  • Born in China, Croft lived in orphanages for ten years after being abandoned by his birth parents when he was two years old. Croft was born with Teratoma and went through several surgeries on his back all while having to switch orphanages due to conditions. After a year in his last orphanage, Croft met his future mom and brother who was in China on a mission trip. Two years later, the family returned to China to adopt Croft and bring him back to Washington. Years later, he discovered Paralympic sport club ParaSport Spokane and met executive director Teresa Skinner who introduced him to basketball and track and field. Since 2013, Croft has traveled all over the world for tournaments and meets
  • Son of Karl and Joni Croft
  • Has two siblings, Brittany and Kyle
  • Enjoys playing with LEGOs, going to movies, hanging out with friends and playing video games
  • is a certified scuba diver through a program called Stay Focused.
Career Highlights
  • ParaPan American Games, Santiago, 2023 (400m T53 - Men, 100m T53 - Men, 5000m T54 - Men, 1500m T54 - Men, 800m T53/54 - Men)

Quick Facts
  • Adopted at age 5 from Ukraine
  • Born with a rare congenital disorder known as popliteal pterygium syndrome, which she inherited from her Ukrainian birth mother. It is believed that her birth mother was exposed to radiation from the Chernobyl nuclear disaster
  • Had her leg amputated from the knee down in May 2011
  • Is a lifeguard
  • Daughter of Chad and Karen Crook
  • Has two brothers, Aaron and Carson, and two sisters, Jenelle and Jenna
  • Hobbies include scuba diving, rock climbing, skiing, camping, traveling, swimming, mentoring kids and trying new sports.
Career Highlights
  • ParaPan American Games, Lima, 2019 (400m Freestyle S9 - Women)

Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Paul and Pauline Crosby and has one brother Darian
  • Married to fellow U.S. Paralympic track and field teammate Erik Hightower
  • Born with albinism that leaves her without pigment in her skin, hair or eyes. She is legally blind with 20/400 vision
  • Is half Jamaican
  • Started in the sport when her brother suggested she join the high school track team after seeing her run the mile in her P.E. class
  • Her motto is "Limited sight, limitless dreams"
  • Loves Hello Kitty and video games
  • Nicknamed “The Flash”
  • Competed for Chico State on the track and field team for three years
  • Hobbies include drawing, watching movies, running, playing video games, cooking and playing with her guide dog Keystone

bio.education: Trabuco Hills High School (Mission Viejo, Calif.) ‘18 San Diego State University ’22, Criminal Justice
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Born with cerebral palsy, Cross has limited balance and no mobility in her right arm. She learned about Paralympic track and field while running at high school meet in California where she met an assistant coach from another school that informed her about events at the California Interscholastic Federation State Track & Field Championships for athletes with physical disabilities. There she met Paralympian Ahkeel Whitehead, founder of the adaptive athletics program at San Diego State University, who brought her into the program. Since then, she has represented Team USA in international events, including the World Para Athletics Junior Championships.
  • Daughter of Melanie and Darrell Cross
  • Has one sister, Emily
  • Born on Leap Day
  • Enjoys listening to music, reading, swimming, going to the beach with her family and playing frisbee with her dad
  • First athlete to compete on the adaptive athletics program at San Diego State University.
Career Highlights
  • ParaPan American Games, Santiago, 2023 (100m T37 - Women, 200m T37 - Women)
  • ParaPan American Games, Lima, 2019 (200m T37 - Women, 100m T37 - Women)

Quick Facts

  • Sport: Track and field

  • Specialty: Shot put

Biographical Information

Ryan Crouser is a world-class shot putter, known for his strength, technique and consistency. Crouser has set multiple world records, with his outdoor shot put personal best of 23.56 meters in 2023. He also excels in indoor competitions, where his personal best of 23.38 meters, set in 2021, broke the longstanding indoor world record.

Crouser comes from a family of Olympic “throwers” including his father and both uncles, who competed in discus, javelin, and shot put, respectively. His two cousins are also javelin throwers. Crouser started his track and field career in middle school. He began setting national records during his sophomore year at the Sam Barlow High School and later attended the University of Texas at Austin. 

A supporter of children’s athletics, Crouser organized a donation event at the Salvation Army to celebrate his 29th birthday, asking all participants to donate athletics equipment for local kids. 

Olympic Highlights

Ryan Crouser became the first athlete ever to win three consecutive gold medals in shot put. His remarkable journey began at the Rio 2016 Olympic Games, where he threw 22.52 meters to set an Olympic record and become the first American man to win a shot put gold medal since 2004.

Crouser's dominance continued at the Tokyo 2020 Olympic Games, where he not only defended his title, but broke his own Olympic record with an incredible throw of 23.30 meters. He completed his historic triple crown at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games, securing gold with a throw of 22.9 meters.

World Championship Highlights

Crouser's world championships career began with a silver medal at Doha 2019, where he achieved a personal best of 22.90 meters. In 2022, he won his first world title in Eugene with a throw of 22.94 meters, whereas earlier that year he earned silver at the world indoor championships. His most prolific world championship performance came in 2023 at Budapest, where he set a record of 23.51 meters to claim gold. He started 2024 by winning his first world indoor championship, adding another prestigious title to his storied collection.

U.S. Championship Highlights 

Ryan Crouser dominated at the national level with his first USA title in 2016, which set him on the path to Olympic success. He continued collecting national honors, winning titles in 2017 and 2019, while placing second in 2018. His most spectacular championship performance came at the 2021 U.S. Olympic trials, where he broke the outdoor world record with a throw of 23.37 meters. In indoor competition, he claimed victory at the 2019 USA indoor championships and secured his fourth national indoor title in 2024.

Early Career

Crouser's early career was marked by significant achievements, even as a junior athlete. He set multiple age-group records in shot put and discus. During his time at the University of Texas, Crouser competed primarily in the shot put event. He won multiple NCAA titles, including three NCAA outdoor championships in the shot put from 2014 to 2016. He set numerous records, including the NCAA all-time best in the shot put. 

Olympic Experience
  • 3-time Olympian; 3-time Olympic medalist (3 gold)
    • Olympic Games Paris 2024, gold (Shot Put - Men)
    • Olympic Games Tokyo 2020, gold (Shot Put - Men)
    • Olympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, gold (Shot Put - Men)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2023 – gold (Shot Put - Men)
  • Years of participation: Shot Put - Men 2017, 2023
  • Medals: 1 (1 gold)
    • Gold – 2023 (Shot Put - Men)
